rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical allylation and allenylation of aldehydes (benzaldehyde) with allylarenes/propargylarenes via cathodically generated allylic/propargylic carbanions under Co-salen/DABCO catalysis
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates a Co(II)-salen / Co(III)-H species that deprotonates the weakly acidic allyl/propargyl C-H of the substrate (HER-type), delivering an allylic or propargylic carbanion that adds to the aldehyde; sacrificial anode oxidation balances charge

key_intermediates.name: alkoxide adduct (3a/5a-alkoxide after C-C bond formation)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Co(II)-salen / generates Co(III)-H that abstracts a proton from the weakly acidic benzylic/propargylic C-H of the substrate (HER-like, mediated), producing an allylic or propargylic carbanion that nucleophilically adds to benzaldehyde to give the homoallylic alcohol (3a) or α-allenyl alcohol (5a); the anode supplies the balancing oxidation.
rate_determining_step: Proton transfer in the HER/deprotonation step generating the carbanion (consistent with observed secondary KIE in parallel experiments)

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted substrates (radical clock 3bm-3br, 5bd, 5be)
probe_reagents.observation: No ring-opening products observed; cyclopropyl group retained in all products, arguing against radical intermediates and supporting an ionic (carbanion) pathway

probe_reagents.name: HOAc
probe_reagents.observation: Suppresses reaction to trace yield — acidic additive preferentially undergoes HER over substrate deprotonation

probe_reagents.name: MeOH
probe_reagents.observation: Reduces yield (32%/12% for 3a/5a Cond II) — competing HER of acidic additive

probe_reagents.name: H2O
probe_reagents.observation: Reduces yield (40%/25%) — competing HER consistent with carbanion mechanism

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Only marginal effect on yield (68-73%), inconsistent with radical chain

probe_reagents.name: P(OEt)3
probe_reagents.observation: Significant yield erosion (21%/48% / 50%) attributed to nucleophilic addition of carbanion to P(OEt)3, supporting carbanion intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: No D incorporation in allylation product 3a; 0.48 D incorporated at allene carbon of 5a — propargylic carbanion has lower reactivity and undergoes reversible H/D exchange at cathode, whereas allylic carbanion adds to aldehyde faster than exchange

probe_reagents.name: 4a-d5 (CD3-labeled propargylarene)
probe_reagents.observation: 5a-d4 product shows 0.25 D at benzylic position and 0.72 D at allene terminus (D depleted from CD3 source) — supports H/D exchange via carbanion

probe_reagents.name: 1be-d2 / 4a-d5 KIE substrates
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel KIE kH/kD = 1.3/1 (allyl) and 1.5/1 (propargyl) — secondary KIE consistent with proton transfer in HER; intermolecular competition gives kH/kD = 3.3/1 and 4.3/1, discrepancy attributed to rapid H/D exchange of the carbanion

notes: DFT (PBE0-D3(BJ)/ma-def2-TZVPP//B3LYP-D3(BJ)/Def2-SVP, SMD DMF) supports nucleophilic addition of allyl/allenyl carbanion to PhCHO: for allylation, the E-pathway is favored (TS1(E) 4.3 kcal/mol; 3a(E) -2.8 kcal/mol) over Z and over a homoallyl regioisomer (TS2 8.9 kcal/mol giving 3a'); for allenylation, addition through the central allenyl C is preferred (TS3 ~1.5-2.6 kcal/mol) giving 5a over the propargylic isomer 5a'. A radical pathway via Co(III)-H HAT was considered but excluded by radical clock and trap experiments; the authors note radical-like Co(III)-H species may still be affected by radical scavengers, explaining the partial yield drop with P(OEt)3.
